rawConfiguration.text:
![Redis Logo](https://redis.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/08/redis-logo.png)
  ### 🔍 **Filter View**
---
⚠️ **Note:** Use the clickable entity names on the widgets marked `Filterable` to isolate your target data.

> ### 📊 **What is this dashboard?**
> This dashboard provides a **real-time** view of your Redis infrastructure — covering command throughput, per-command latency, memory utilization, client connections, keyspace health, and persistence. Use it to detect performance bottlenecks, memory pressure, and connection issues before they impact your applications.
>

> ### 👥 **Who is this for?**
> * **DevOps/SRE:** To monitor memory saturation, connection limits, and replication lag.
> * **Developers:** To identify slow commands blocking the single-threaded event loop.
> * **Product Owners:** To understand cache effectiveness (hit ratio) and system availability.
>

> ### 🎯 Key Signals
> | Signal | Healthy | Warning | Critical |
> |--------|---------|---------|----------|
> | Hit Ratio | > 80% | 50-80% | < 50% |
> | Memory % | < 70% | 70-90% | > 90% |
> | Fragmentation | 1.0-1.5 | 1.5-2.0 | > 2.0 or < 1.0 |
> | Rejected Conn. | 0 | — | > 0 |

> ### 🔗 **Quick Navigation**
> * [**New Relic Redis OTel Documentation**](https://docs.newrelic.com/docs/opentelemetry/integrations/redis/redis-otel-integration-overview/)
> * [**Metric Reference**](https://docs.newrelic.com/docs/opentelemetry/integrations/redis/metrics-reference/)
> * [**Troubleshoot and get support**](https://docs.newrelic.com/docs/opentelemetry/integrations/redis/troubleshooting/)

widgets.title: Uptime
widgets.description: How long Redis has been running since the last restart. A sudden reset indicates a crash or manual restart that may have caused data loss.

nrqlQueries.query: SELECT latest(redis.uptime)/3600 AS 'Hours' FROM Metric WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' COMPARE WITH 1 hour ago

widgets.title: Commands/sec
widgets.description: Current throughput of commands per second. A sudden drop may indicate client connectivity issues or a slow command blocking the event loop.

nrqlQueries.query: SELECT rate(sum(redis.commands.processed), 1 second) AS 'commands/sec' FROM Metric WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' COMPARE WITH  1 hour ago

widgets.title: Hit Ratio %
widgets.description: Percentage of key lookups that found data. A low ratio means clients are requesting expired/evicted keys, causing a fallback to slower data sources.

nrqlQueries.query: SELECT (sum(redis.keyspace.hits)/(sum(redis.keyspace.hits)+sum(redis.keyspace.misses)))*100 AS 'Hit Ratio%' FROM Metric  WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' COMPARE WITH  1 hour ago

widgets.title: Memory Used
widgets.description: Total memory allocated by Redis. Approaching maxmemory triggers evictions which degrade cache hit rates and increase latency.

nrqlQueries.query: SELECT latest(redis.memory.used)/1048576 AS 'MB' FROM Metric WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' COMPARE WITH 1 hour ago

widgets.title: Connected Clients
widgets.description: Number of active client connections. Deviations from the normal range indicate upstream connection pool issues or potential connection leaks.

nrqlQueries.query: SELECT latest(redis.clients.connected) FROM Metric WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' COMPARE WITH 1 hour ago

widgets.title: Connected Replicas
widgets.description: Number of replica instances connected to this primary. An unexpected drop means a replica went down, reducing read capacity and fault tolerance.

nrqlQueries.query: SELECT latest(redis.slaves.connected) FROM Metric WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' COMPARE WITH 1 hour ago

rawConfiguration.text:
# ⚡ Performance & Throughput
> 💡 **Watch for:** Commands/sec dropping while latency rises = slow command blocking the event loop. Check `Command Latency p99` to identify the culprit.

widgets.title: Commands Processed/sec
widgets.description: Total command throughput over time. Correlate drops with latency spikes to identify slow commands or network saturation.

nrqlQueries.query: SELECT rate(sum(redis.commands.processed), 1 second) FROM Metric WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' TIMESERIES

widgets.title: Keyspace Hit Ratio
widgets.description: Cache effectiveness trend. A declining ratio suggests TTL misconfiguration, insufficient memory, or workload pattern changes.

nrqlQueries.query: SELECT (sum(redis.keyspace.hits)/(sum(redis.keyspace.hits)+sum(redis.keyspace.misses)))*100 FROM Metric WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' TIMESERIES

widgets.title: Command Calls by Type
widgets.description: Which commands are called most frequently. Identifies hot commands (e.g., excessive KEYS or SCAN) that may degrade performance.

nrqlQueries.query: SELECT rate(sum(redis.cmd.calls), 1 second) FROM Metric WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' FACET cmd LIMIT 10 TIMESERIES

widgets.title: Command Latency p99
widgets.description: Tail latency per command type. Slow commands block the single-threaded event loop and increase latency for all subsequent requests.

nrqlQueries.query: SELECT max(redis.cmd.latency) FROM Metric WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' AND percentile='p99' FACET cmd LIMIT 10 TIMESERIES

widgets.title: Network I/O
widgets.description: Bytes flowing in and out per second. Sustained high throughput may saturate the network link.

nrqlQueries.query: SELECT rate(sum(redis.net.input), 1 second) AS 'Input bytes/sec', rate(sum(redis.net.output), 1 second) AS 'Output bytes/sec' FROM Metric WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' TIMESERIES

widgets.title: Commands/sec by Instance
widgets.description: Command throughput broken down by Redis instance. Identifies which instance is handling the most load — useful for detecting unbalanced traffic or a hot instance in a fleet.

nrqlQueries.query: SELECT rate(sum(redis.commands.processed), 1 second) FROM Metric WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' FACET entity.name TIMESERIES

rawConfiguration.text:
# 🧠 Memory
  > ⚠️  **Pressure chain:** Memory hits limit → Evictions start → Hit ratio drops → App latency increases. If `Fragmentation Ratio` > 1.5, consider restarting
  Redis to reclaim fragmented memory.

widgets.title: Memory Used vs Maxmemory
widgets.description: How close usage is to the configured limit. When used hits maxmemory, Redis begins evicting keys

nrqlQueries.query: SELECT latest(redis.memory.used) AS 'Used', latest(redis.maxmemory) AS 'Max' FROM Metric WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' TIMESERIES

widgets.title: Fragmentation Ratio
widgets.description: Ratio of OS-allocated to Redis-allocated memory. >1.5 means excessive fragmentation; <1 means swapping to disk.

nrqlQueries.query: SELECT latest(redis.memory.fragmentation_ratio) FROM Metric WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' TIMESERIES

widgets.title: Memory Breakdown
widgets.description: Comparison of memory measurements. Peak shows historical max; RSS shows actual OS allocation.

nrqlQueries.query: SELECT latest(redis.memory.used) AS 'Used', latest(redis.memory.rss) AS 'RSS', latest(redis.memory.peak) AS 'Peak', latest(redis.memory.lua) AS 'Lua' FROM Metric WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' TIMESERIES

widgets.title: Evictions & Expirations
widgets.description: Rate of keys being removed. High evictions mean memory pressure is forcing data loss.

nrqlQueries.query: SELECT rate(sum(redis.keys.evicted), 1 second) AS 'Evicted/sec', rate(sum(redis.keys.expired), 1 second) AS 'Expired/sec' FROM Metric WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' TIMESERIES

rawConfiguration.text:
# 🔌 Clients, Connections & Keyspace
  > 💡 **Watch for:** `Rejected Connections > 0` means `maxclients` limit is hit — immediate action needed. Steadily growing connections with no plateau =
  connection leak in your application.

widgets.title: Connected & Blocked Clients
widgets.description: Active connections vs clients waiting on blocking operations. Rising blocked clients indicates empty source lists or upstream delays.

nrqlQueries.query: SELECT latest(redis.clients.connected) AS 'Connected', latest(redis.clients.blocked) AS 'Blocked' FROM Metric WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' TIMESERIES

widgets.title: Connections Received & Rejected
widgets.description: New connection rate vs rejections. Rejections occur when maxclients limit is hit

nrqlQueries.query: SELECT rate(sum(redis.connections.received), 1 second) AS 'Received/sec', rate(sum(redis.connections.rejected), 1 second) AS 'Rejected/sec' FROM Metric WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' TIMESERIES

widgets.title: Connected Clients by Instance
widgets.description: Client connections per Redis instance. Reveals connection imbalance across instances — one instance with significantly more clients may indicate misconfigured connection pooling or DNS routing issues.

nrqlQueries.query: SELECT latest(redis.clients.connected) FROM Metric WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' FACET entity.name TIMESERIES

widgets.title: Keys by Database
widgets.description: Key count across databases. Flat-lining at maxmemory with evictions means the cache is at capacity.

nrqlQueries.query: SELECT latest(redis.db.keys) FROM Metric WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' FACET db TIMESERIES

widgets.title: Keyspace Hits vs Misses
widgets.description: Raw hit/miss rates. Spikes in misses correlate with deployments, TTL changes, or eviction events.

nrqlQueries.query: SELECT rate(sum(redis.keyspace.hits), 1 second) AS 'Hits/sec', rate(sum(redis.keyspace.misses), 1 second) AS 'Misses/sec' FROM Metric WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' TIMESERIES

widgets.title: Keys per DB
widgets.description: Snapshot of each Redis database (db0, db1, etc.) showing total keys, keys with TTL set, and average TTL. Identifies databases with no expiration configured (risk of unbounded growth).

nrqlQueries.query: SELECT latest(redis.db.keys) AS 'Total Keys', latest(redis.db.expires) AS 'With TTL', latest(redis.db.avg_ttl)/1000 AS 'Avg TTL (sec)' FROM Metric WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' FACET db

rawConfiguration.text:
# 💾 System & Persistence
  > 💡 **Watch for:** Spikes in `sys_children` on CPU Time = background save (BGSAVE) running. Correlate with `Latest Fork Duration` — if fork exceeds 1 second,
   your dataset may be too large for the available memory. High `RDB Changes Since Last Save` means more data at risk if the server crashes before the next
  snapshot.

widgets.title: CPU Time by State
widgets.description: CPU consumed by Redis broken down by state. Spikes in sys_children indicate background save operations consuming resources.

nrqlQueries.query: SELECT rate(sum(redis.cpu.time), 1 second) FROM Metric WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' FACET state TIMESERIES

widgets.title: RDB Changes Since Last Save
widgets.description: Write operations since last snapshot. High values mean more data at risk of loss if the server crashes.

nrqlQueries.query: SELECT latest(redis.rdb.changes_since_last_save) FROM Metric WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' TIMESERIES

widgets.title: Latest Fork Duration
widgets.description: Time taken for last fork() for background saves. Long fork times cause latency spikes proportional to dataset size.

nrqlQueries.query: SELECT latest(redis.latest_fork) FROM Metric WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' TIMESERIES

widgets.title: Replication Offset
widgets.description: Primary's replication stream position. Comparing with replica offsets reveals replication lag.

nrqlQueries.query: SELECT latest(`redis.replication.offset`) FROM Metric WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' AND metricName = 'redis.replication.offset' TIMESERIES

widgets.title: Network I/O by Instance
widgets.description: Network bytes in/out per Redis instance. High output on a single instance may indicate large value retrievals (bulk GET) or replication sync traffic to replicas.

nrqlQueries.query: SELECT rate(sum(redis.net.input), 1 second) AS 'In', rate(sum(redis.net.output), 1 second) AS 'Out' FROM Metric WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' FACET entity.name TIMESERIES LIMIT 25

rawConfiguration.text:
# 🧩 Cluster Health
  > 💡 **Note:** This section requires the **Prometheus receiver** (using redis_exporter) and will display data only when cluster monitoring is enabled and Redis is running in cluster mode. Standalone mode will not display data.

widgets.title: Cluster State
widgets.description: Overall cluster health. OK = all slots served, FAIL = slots unavailable.

nrqlQueries.query: FROM Metric SELECT IF(latest(redis.cluster.state) = 1, 'OK', 'FAIL') AS 'Cluster State' WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' AND metricName = 'redis.cluster.state'

widgets.title: Cluster Size
widgets.description: Number of master nodes serving hash slots.

nrqlQueries.query: FROM Metric SELECT latest(redis.cluster.size) AS 'Masters' WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' AND metricName = 'redis.cluster.size'

widgets.title: Known Nodes
widgets.description: Total nodes (masters + replicas) in the cluster.

nrqlQueries.query: FROM Metric SELECT latest(redis.cluster.known_nodes) AS 'Nodes' WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' AND metricName = 'redis.cluster.known_nodes'

widgets.title: Slots OK
widgets.description: Slots actively serving requests. Must be 16384 in a healthy cluster.

nrqlQueries.query: FROM Metric SELECT latest(redis.cluster.slots_ok) AS 'OK' WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' AND metricName = 'redis.cluster.slots_ok'

widgets.title: Slots PFail
widgets.description: Slots suspected unreachable (early warning).

nrqlQueries.query: FROM Metric SELECT latest(redis.cluster.slots_pfail) AS 'PFail' WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' AND metricName = 'redis.cluster.slots_pfail'

widgets.title: Slots Fail
widgets.description: Slots in confirmed failure — NOT serving traffic.

nrqlQueries.query: FROM Metric SELECT latest(redis.cluster.slots_fail) AS 'Fail' WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' AND metricName = 'redis.cluster.slots_fail'

widgets.title: Slot Health Over Time
widgets.description: Track slot states to detect degradation patterns.

nrqlQueries.query: FROM Metric SELECT latest(redis.cluster.slots_ok) AS 'OK', latest(redis.cluster.slots_pfail) AS 'PFail', latest(redis.cluster.slots_fail) AS 'Fail' WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' AND metricName IN ('redis.cluster.slots_ok','redis.cluster.slots_pfail','redis.cluster.slots_fail') TIMESERIES AUTO

widgets.title: Cluster Nodes Over Time
widgets.description: Track node membership changes over time.

nrqlQueries.query: FROM Metric SELECT latest(redis.cluster.known_nodes) AS 'Known Nodes', latest(redis.cluster.size) AS 'Masters' WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' AND metricName IN ('redis.cluster.known_nodes','redis.cluster.size') TIMESERIES AUTO

widgets.title: Config Epoch
widgets.description: Cluster configuration version. Rapid increases indicate instability.

nrqlQueries.query: FROM Metric SELECT latest(redis.cluster.current_epoch) AS 'Config Epoch' WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' AND metricName = 'redis.cluster.current_epoch' TIMESERIES AUTO

widgets.title: Cluster Bus Messages Sent
widgets.description: Gossip traffic sent. A flat line may indicate network isolation.

nrqlQueries.query: FROM Metric SELECT latest(redis.cluster.messages_sent) AS 'Messages Sent' WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' AND metricName = 'redis.cluster.messages_sent' TIMESERIES AUTO

widgets.title: Cluster Bus Messages Received
widgets.description: Gossip messages received. A drop indicates possible partition.

nrqlQueries.query: FROM Metric SELECT latest(redis.cluster.messages_received) AS 'Messages Received' WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' AND metricName = 'redis.cluster.messages_received' TIMESERIES AUTO

widgets.title: Cluster Bus Connections
widgets.description: Inter-node TCP connections. Should be (N-1) where N is total nodes.

nrqlQueries.query: FROM Metric SELECT latest(redis.cluster.connections) AS 'Bus Connections' WHERE instrumentation.provider = 'opentelemetry' AND metricName = 'redis.cluster.connections' TIMESERIES AUTO
